target.title: [Feature]: Channel Broker Phase 2A conformance harness
target.body:
## Maintainer TL;DR This stack consolidates the recurring Telegram/Discord/Slack/WhatsApp/Signal/iMessage/etc. maintenance churn behind one Channel Broker contract. OpenClaw keeps the shared semantics that repeatedly regress across native channel plugins: sessions, allowlists, routing, /verbose, streaming policy, durable final sends, receipts, retries, and auditability. Broker providers own platform mechanics: bot APIs, bridge/device state, native ids, delivery quirks, and provider capabilities. This PR is the regression shield. It turns the recurring channel pain into shared conformance: signed inbound webhooks, normalization, dedupe, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, preview/progress gates, and /verbose channelData. The direction is intentionally incremental: first land the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, then prove conformance, then prove opt-in routing for the major channels, then model long-tail and constrained providers. Only after migration paths exist should legacy native-channel feature work freeze in #86115. ## Why This Is Better Today, one fix to streaming, /verbose, or delivery behavior can make Telegram fast this week and then break Discord, Slack, or another native plugin the next week. The broker path makes those shared semantics one OpenClaw-owned contract instead of many platform-specific re-implementations. When a provider adds support for previews, threads, receipts, media, or constrained account modes, that capability can become available through the same contract to routed channels whose provider declares and passes conformance for it. ## Stack Shape ```mermaid flowchart TD U["Umbrella issue #86105: reduce maintained-channel churn"] --> P1["#86096 Phase 1: SDK + bundled broker foundation"] P1 --> P2A["#86153 Phase 2A: conformance + signed inbound"] P2A --> P2B["#86154 Phase 2B: Telegram opt-in proof"] P2B --> P2C["#86156 Phase 2C: Discord opt-in proof"] P2C --> P2D["#86157 Phase 2D: Slack opt-in proof"] P2D --> P3["#86164 Phase 3: official/app capability matrix"] P3 --> P4["#86165 Phase 4: constrained provider capabilities"] P4 -. "future, not in this stack" .-> P5["Future freeze issue #86115: legacy native-plugin freeze"] ``` | PR | Role in the consolidation | | --- | --- | | #86096 | Creates the versioned broker SDK/plugin foundation, provider routing guard, media final-send mapping, SecretRef/cancellation handling, prefix-ownership contract, and truthful declared-capability boundary. | | #86153 | Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff: inbound dedupe, explicit listed-provider gating, durable final sends, receipts, retryable failures, payload/live-preview delivery requests, and /verbose channelData. | | #86154 | Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. | | #86156 | Proves Discord DMs/channels/threads preserve conversation semantics through broker routing. | | #86157 | Proves Slack DMs/channel threads preserve direct/thread semantics through broker routing. | | #86164 | Adds official/app and regional platform aliases, inbound alias canonicalization, WeChat/Weixin coverage, capability matrix rows, and declared delivery capability enforcement for long-tail channels. | | #86165 | Adds closed constrained-provider metadata for WhatsApp, Signal, iMessage, and bridge-backed providers, including BlueBubbles-backed Mac/Messages constraints, without making BlueBubbles a separate platform identity. | ## Ownership Model ```mermaid flowchart LR Core["OpenClaw core
sessions, routing, allowlists
/verbose, streaming, retries
receipts, audit"] --> Broker["channel-broker plugin
openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker
V1 protocol"] Broker --> Provider["Provider-owned broker
HTTP/webhook transport
platform adapters"] Provider --> Platforms["Telegram, Discord, Slack
Teams, Matrix, WhatsApp
Signal, iMessage, long tail"] Provider --> Broker Broker --> Core ``` ## Review Shape These PRs are intentionally linear. GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 2A - Conformance and signed inbound baseline - Role: Proves shared lifecycle conformance and adds signed inbound webhook registration/runtime handoff, payload/live-preview delivery requests, /verbose channelData preservation, and explicit listed-provider gating. - Head: `3f295c3742` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `3f295c3742` This branch also carries the tiny `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timeout hardening inherited from #86096 after the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` shard repeatedly timed out there while the single file passed locally. Focused local validation after the latest package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `pnpm exec tsc -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json --noEmit` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 2A Channel Broker conformance baseline on top of #86096. - Extends `openclaw/plugin-sdk/channel-broker` with inbound normalization, dedupe-key, provider capability normalization, and platform capability checks. - Adds bundled broker conformance coverage for durable inbound receive, durable final send receipts, retryable receipt failures, preview/progress capability gates, and `/verbose`/tool-message `channelData`. ## Rollout Context Closes #86109 Related #86108 Related #86105 Stacked on #86096 Supersedes closed PR #86133, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. This PR intentionally does not migrate Telegram, Discord, or Slack routing. Native defaults stay unchanged until the Phase 2B/2C/2D proofs land behind opt-in broker routing. For review, the focused stack diff is: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** The broker conformance baseline normalizes inbound events, builds durable dedupe keys, evaluates preview/progress capabilities, and sends outbound requests through the bundled broker lifecycle without platform-specific core branches. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that imported `src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts`, created a Telegram inbound event, built the durable dedupe key, evaluated Discord preview/progress capabilities, and sent broker outbound requests through `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text`. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "inboundDedupeKey": "acme:bot-main:telegram:live-proof-telegram-1", "previewProgressSupported": true } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live SDK/plugin path produced the expected durable inbound key and capability decision outside the Vitest harness, proving the Phase 2A contract surface can carry the normalized lifecycle facts that later channel migrations consume. - **What was not tested:** Live provider network traffic and the full local suite; those remain covered by later provider proofs and GitHub Actions. ### Behavior The broker contract now has a conformance harness for the lifecycle paths that regress most often across maintained chat channels: inbound normalization/dedupe, durable final sends, provider receipts, retry/failure propagation, preview/progress capability checks, and `/verbose` tool-message metadata. ### Environment - Local checkout: `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker` - Branch: `feat/channel-broker-conformance` - Head: `3f295c3742` (current Phase 2A head) ### Steps 1. Normalized a synthetic Telegram inbound event through the SDK and accepted it through the durable inbound receive journal. 2. Replayed the same dedupe key while pending and after completion to prove duplicate/redelivery handling. 3. Sent a synthetic Slack final message through the bundled broker runtime and verified provider receipt commit material. 4. Returned a retryable Discord provider receipt and verified the send fails with structured receipt context. 5. Built preview/finalize broker requests and asserted Discord preview/progress support through declared capabilities. 6. Passed `/verbose` tool-message policy through broker `channelData` without platform-specific branching. ### Evidence - `OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/Volumes/LEXAR/Codex/channel-broker-vitest-include.json pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts` - 1 file passed, 7 tests passed. - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/secret-contract.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - 3 files passed, 10 tests passed. - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - passed. - `pnpm tsgo:core:test` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.ts src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/outbound.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - passed. - `git diff --check` - passed. ### Observed Result The SDK helpers normalize provider payloads without dropping native metadata, the bundled plugin refuses non-sent provider receipts as durable-send failures, and conformance tests prove the current broker path can represent the Phase 2 lifecycle requirements without changing user-visible channel defaults. ### Not Tested - Full local suite. Per `/Volumes/LEXAR` policy, broad validation is left to GitHub Actions. - Live Telegram, Discord, or Slack provider traffic. Those are intentionally scoped to Phase 2B/2C/2D. ## Current Stack Gate Context This branch is rebased on the current Phase 1 foundation head. Focused local validation is documented above or below; broad validation remains on GitHub Actions.

GitHub still shows `main` as the base for each PR so maintainers can land them one by one from fork branches; the branches themselves are stacked, and maintainers can review the focused adjacent diffs here: | PR | Focused adjacent diff | | --- | --- | | #86153 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-protocol...feat/channel-broker-conformance | | #86154 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing | | #86156 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing...feat/channel-broker-discord-routing | | #86157 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-discord-routing...feat/channel-broker-slack-routing | | #86164 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-slack-routing...feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities | | #86165 | https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-official-platform-capabilities...feat/channel-broker-constrained-platform-capabilities | ## This PR - Phase: Phase 2B - Telegram opt-in broker routing proof - Role: Proves Telegram DMs and topics can route through broker targets without native Telegram internals. - Head: `d1de4ad7c8` ## Current Review State This PR is live for maintainer review. Current head: `d1de4ad7c8` This branch also carries the tiny `src/agents/model-catalog-visibility.test.ts` timeout hardening inherited from #86096 after the unrelated `checks-node-agentic-agents` shard repeatedly timed out there while the single file passed locally. Focused local validation after the latest package/deadcode/doc/capability repair and upstream/main rebase ran from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`: - `git diff --check upstream/main..HEAD` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugin-sdk/channel-broker.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/config-schema.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/http-routes.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-package-contract-guardrails.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.contracts-plugin.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/bundled-plugin-build-entries.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.tooling.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `pnpm exec vitest run test/scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.unit-fast.config.ts --maxWorkers=1` - `node scripts/check-deadcode-unused-files.mjs` - `pnpm exec tsc -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json --noEmit` Broad validation remains owned by GitHub Actions. ## Summary - Adds the Phase 2B Telegram broker routing proof on top of #86153 and #86096. - Normalizes Telegram topic shorthand in the broker plugin, including `broker:telegram::topic:` and numeric `:` targets. - Canonicalizes outbound broker session routes to `telegram:?threadId=` so topic routing is stable without importing native Telegram internals. ## Rollout Context Closes #86110 Related #86108 Related #86105 Stacked on #86153 Stacked on #86096 Supersedes closed PR #86137, which was closed by the repository active-PR cap rather than by an implementation failure. Native Telegram defaults stay unchanged. Existing target-prefix ownership still keeps `telegram:` owned by the native Telegram plugin when that plugin is installed; this PR proves the opt-in broker route through `broker:telegram:...` and the broker alias path when native Telegram is absent. For review, the focused stack diff is: https://github.com/100yenadmin/openclaw/compare/feat/channel-broker-conformance...feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing ## Real Behavior Proof - **Behavior or issue addressed:** Telegram topic targets can route through `channel-broker` and become provider requests with canonical `telegram` platform, channel conversation id, and topic `threadId`. - **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout at `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker`, using the Channel Broker plugin and SDK from the stacked rollout branch on May 24, 2026. - **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a `node --import tsx --input-type=module` terminal proof that called `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text` with `to: "broker:telegram:-1001234567890:topic:42"` and captured the provider request passed to the configured broker runtime. - **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output excerpt from that local command: ```json { "label": "telegram-topic", "messageId": "native-telegram-topic", "platform": "telegram", "conversation": { "id": "-1001234567890", "type": "channel", "threadId": "42" }, "requirements": { "text": true, "thread": true } } ``` - **Observed result after fix:** The live plugin path normalized Telegram topic shorthand into the broker provider request shape without importing native Telegram extension internals. - **What was not tested:** Live Telegram Bot API traffic and the full local suite; this PR stays scoped to opt-in broker routing proof. ### Behavior OpenClaw can route a Telegram topic through `channel-broker` without Telegram extension code. The broker receives a normalized platform `telegram`, conversation id, and thread id; OpenClaw keeps the session route canonical and leaves native Telegram prefix ownership intact. ### Environment - Local checkout: `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-channel-broker` - Branch: `feat/channel-broker-telegram-routing` - Head: `d1de4ad7c8` (current Telegram proof head) ### Steps 1. Sent a synthetic message to `broker:telegram:-1001234567890:topic:42` through `channelBrokerPlugin.message.send.text`. 2. Verified the provider request used `platform: "telegram"`, `conversation.id: "-1001234567890"`, and `conversation.threadId: "42"`. 3. Resolved the outbound session route for the same target. 4. Verified the route canonicalized to `telegram:-1001234567890?threadId=42` and preserved channel chat semantics. ### Evidence - `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/conformance.test.ts --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-channel-broker.config.ts` - 2 files passed, 10 tests passed. - `pnpm exec tsc --noEmit -p extensions/channel-broker/tsconfig.json` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check extensions/channel-broker/src/target.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.ts extensions/channel-broker/src/channel.test.ts` - passed. - `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/channel-broker --format unix` - passed. - `git diff --check` - passed. ### Observed Result Telegram topic routing is represented by broker-owned target normalization and provider request semantics, not by core importing or calling native Telegram extension internals. ### Not Tested - Live Telegram Bot API traffic; this PR is the opt-in broker migration proof, not a live provider connector. - Full local suite. Per `/Volumes/LEXAR` policy, broad validation is left to GitHub Actions. ## Current Stack Gate Context This branch is rebased on the current Phase 1 foundation head. Focused local validation is documented above or below; broad validation remains on GitHub Actions.

target.title: fix(plugin-state): evict current namespace on plugin row cap
target.body:
## Summary

- Make plugin-state shed old rows from the namespace that just grew before failing a plugin-wide row-limit write.
- Raise the plugin-state per-plugin row budget and Telegram persistent message-cache budget from 1000 to 3000 rows.
- Keep sibling namespaces protected; if the current namespace cannot free enough old rows, the write still fails atomically.
- Add regression coverage for the Telegram shape that failed in production, now sized against the 3000-row budget.

## Verification

All commands below were rerun on rebased head `feee577cb1` using the existing system Node install: `node v26.2.0` at `/usr/bin/node`.

- `node -v && which node` -> `v26.2.0`, `/usr/bin/node`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.sqlite.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.test.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.e2e.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.test.ts --reporter=dot --passWithNoTests=false` (`23 passed`)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.e2e.test.ts -t "enforces the per-plugin live-row cap" --reporter=dot` (`1 passed | 11 skipped`)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts --reporter=dot --passWithNoTests=false` (`16 passed`)
- `pnpm run tsgo:core`
- `pnpm run tsgo:test:src`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram plugin state reached the old 1000 live-row plugin budget (message-cache plus topic-name-cache), so a new topic/message cache write threw `PluginStateStoreError` and surfaced as a generic Telegram processing failure.

Budget after fix: plugin-state allows 3000 live rows per plugin, and Telegram persistent message-cache allows 3000 entries.

Real runtime probe: On rebased PR head `feee577cb1`, I ran a `tsx` probe with system Node `v26.2.0` at `/usr/bin/node` against a temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, using the actual `createPluginStateKeyedStore` runtime API and the real plugin-state SQLite store. The probe seeded 2989 `telegram.message-cache` rows plus 11 `telegram.topic-name-cache` rows, then registered one new message-cache row.

Runtime output:

```text
NODE_VERSION=v26.2.0
NODE_PATH=/usr/bin/node
PR_HEAD=feee577cb1
STATE_DIR=/tmp/<temp>
DB_PATH=$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/plugin-state/state.sqlite
PLUGIN_CAP=3000
SEEDED_BEFORE message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000
REGISTER_NEW result=success key=msg-new
AFTER message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000
EVICTED_ORIGINAL_MESSAGE_KEYS=msg-0000
ASSERT new_message_present=true
ASSERT exactly_one_original_message_evicted=true
ASSERT topic_cache_preserved=true
ASSERT plugin_total_within_cap=true
DB_BYTES=585728
```

Evidence after fix: The real runtime probe successfully wrote the new Telegram message-cache row at the 3000-row plugin budget, evicted exactly one older message-cache row from the current namespace, preserved all 11 topic-name-cache rows, and kept the plugin total within cap.

Observed result after fix: Current-namespace cache growth no longer fails only because sibling plugin-state namespaces are also using rows, and the Telegram cache has a larger 3000-row runway.

What was not tested: A full live Telegram gateway rebuild on this PR head; production was manually unblocked by trimming old Telegram message-cache rows before this patch. The after-fix path was tested with the real plugin-state runtime API and SQLite store in an isolated runtime state directory.

target.title: fix(cli): bound local CLI lifetime after completion and finite timeout
target.body:
## Problem

`openclaw agent --local` can finish the agent turn and still keep the CLI process alive indefinitely. The completed command is visible in stdout, but ref'd background handles can prevent Node from exiting.

PR #70691, merged on 2026-04-23 by @Takhoffman, fixed the gateway WebSocket teardown path by waiting for gateway client shutdown. This PR covers the remaining local-agent CLI lifetime gap where work has completed but non-gateway handles, such as compaction loops, telemetry exporter timers, or plugin loops, still keep the process alive.

## Change and value

This is an availability workaround for `agent --local`, not a full lifecycle cleanup of every background subsystem.

- Adds a local-agent lifetime helper for CLI entry points.
- After a completed `agent --local` command, drains stdout/stderr, calls the optional otel pre-exit hook if registered, then exits with `process.exitCode ?? 0`.
- Arms a 3s SIGKILL fallback before awaiting the otel hook so a hung flush cannot hang the CLI forever.
- Adds a wall-clock hard timeout only when the operator passed an explicit positive `--timeout N`; `--timeout 0` disables it, and omitted `--timeout` does not arm a new hard kill.

The otel contract is intentionally optional:

```ts
globalThis[Symbol.for("openclaw.otel.preExit")]
```

If the hook is absent or from an older plugin, the CLI falls through to immediate forced exit. If the hook rejects, the CLI still exits.

## Who is affected, and who is not

Affected:

- `openclaw agent --local ...` runs where the agent turn completes but ref'd background handles keep the process alive.
- Automation that already supplies `--timeout N` and needs a bounded outer CLI lifetime if the command promise itself never resolves.

Not affected:

- Non-local agent runs.
- Help/version invocations.
- Other CLI subcommands.
- Local-agent runs without explicit `--timeout`; these still get the post-completion forced exit, but no new wall-clock kill is added. That preserves the existing agent timeout contract, including the configured/default agent timeout path.

## Why now

#86264 and #86276 were aimed closer to the root cause, but that route grew into cross-subsystem lifecycle work and async config import hazards. This PR deliberately takes the smaller, easier-to-review path: release the caller once the local agent work is done, while leaving deeper subsystem teardown work to follow-up fixes.

Once the root cause is fixed, this becomes mostly redundant: the post-completion exit fires after successful work, and the wall-clock timer never matters if the promise resolves. The main residual risk is that `process.exit()` bypasses future arbitrary exit-time drains; this PR handles the known telemetry case through the optional pre-exit hook.

## Relationship to related PRs

- #70691 fixed the gateway WebSocket teardown path and is already merged.
- #86264 and #86276 explored broader/root-cause cleanup paths but are not the approach taken here.
- This PR is scoped to local-agent CLI lifetime behavior and keeps the root-cause cleanup concern explicit.

## Verification

- Local, after rebasing onto `main` at `96bd939995873d6e6a24fe7d0e6e7779e669c8ff`:
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/cli/local-agent-lifetime.ts src/cli/local-agent-lifetime.test.ts src/entry.ts src/index.ts`
  - `timeout 180s pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/local-agent-lifetime.test.ts --reporter=verbose` (`14 passed`)
  - `pnpm tsgo:core`
- Crabbox/GCP exact-head validation:
  - GCP Spot, `us-east1-b`, `e2-standard-8`
  - Node `v24.15.0`, npm `11.12.1`, pnpm `11.2.2`
  - Base `96bd939995873d6e6a24fe7d0e6e7779e669c8ff`
  - Head `9549c867a64fd87fdb9edba89142c9ce492dae9a`
  - `PR87097_HEAD_VALIDATION=passed`
  - `PR87097_BEHAVIOR_PROOF=passed`
- Post-proof rebase:
  - `main` advanced again while the full proof was running.
  - Rebased onto `ebe09be500c50a2708750d8c51235c2f24a828f4`, producing head `571349c9d155efd86f84fe627d17a1b005b06e6e`.
  - No PR-owned files changed across the rebase; it picked up an upstream lint fix outside this PR.
  - Rechecked direct core oxlint, `oxfmt`, focused Vitest (`14 passed`), and `pnpm tsgo:core` on `571349c9d155efd86f84fe627d17a1b005b06e6e`.
- Review gates:
  - Subagent review: no actionable findings.
  - Claude review: no blocking findings; static argv option-list drift and SIGKILL fallback lifetime were triaged as maintenance/intentional residual risks.
  - Codex review: flagged omitted-timeout hard kill as a concern; triaged as intentional because this PR now preserves the configured/default agent timeout contract unless the operator explicitly passed `--timeout N`.

AI-assisted: yes.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: `openclaw agent --local` can complete the agent turn and still not exit because a ref'd background handle keeps the Node process alive. The patch forces exit after completed local-agent work, while flushing the optional otel pre-exit hook first.

Real environment tested: Crabbox on GCP Spot `us-east1-b`, `e2-standard-8`; Node `v24.15.0`; npm `11.12.1`; pnpm `11.2.2`; main at proof time `96bd939995873d6e6a24fe7d0e6e7779e669c8ff`; PR head `9549c867a64fd87fdb9edba89142c9ce492dae9a`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Built current main and the PR head from fresh shallow checkouts, then ran the real CLI entry point with a ref'd preload handle plus an otel pre-exit hook. The after-patch command was:

```bash
timeout 90s env HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr87097-proof/after-pr/home OPENCLAW_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pr87097-proof/after-pr/home/.openclaw OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-pr87097-proof/after-pr/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-pr87097-proof/after-pr/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=qa-mock-not-a-real-key NODE_DISABLE_COMPILE_CACHE=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--import=/tmp/openclaw-pr87097-proof/after-pr/openclaw-pr87097-ref-handle-and-otel.mjs OPENCLAW_PR87097_OTEL_PROOF_FILE=/tmp/openclaw-pr87097-proof/after-pr/output/otel-pre-exit.log node /tmp/openclaw-pr87097-proof/after-pr/repo/openclaw.mjs agent --local --agent main --session-id pr87097-after-pr-1779860799 --message Return marker OPENCLAW_PR87097_AFTER_PR_OK --thinking off --json
```

Evidence after fix: Terminal output from the exact-head Crabbox run:

```text
BASE_REF_REQUESTED=96bd939995873d6e6a24fe7d0e6e7779e669c8ff
PATCHED_REF_REQUESTED=9549c867a64fd87fdb9edba89142c9ce492dae9a

===== PR87097 before-main scenario =====
REF=96bd939995873d6e6a24fe7d0e6e7779e669c8ff
OPENCLAW_VERSION=OpenClaw 2026.5.26 (96bd939)
EXIT_CODE=124
ELAPSED_SEC=92
ASSERT_EXIT_CODE=passed expected=124 actual=124
ASSERT_MARKER_IN_AGENT_LOG=passed
ASSERT_MOCK_REQUEST_LOG=passed
ASSERT_OTEL_PRE_EXIT_HOOK=skipped-before-hook-not-called
ASSERT_AGENT_TURN=skipped-timeout-preserved-before-hang
PR87097_before-main_SCENARIO=passed

===== PR87097 after-pr scenario =====
REF=9549c867a64fd87fdb9edba89142c9ce492dae9a
OPENCLAW_VERSION=OpenClaw 2026.5.26 (9549c86)
EXIT_CODE=0
ELAPSED_SEC=14
ASSERT_EXIT_CODE=passed expected=0 actual=0
ASSERT_MARKER_IN_AGENT_LOG=passed
ASSERT_MOCK_REQUEST_LOG=passed
ASSERT_OTEL_PRE_EXIT_HOOK=passed
ASSERT_AGENT_TURN=passed
PR87097_after-pr_SCENARIO=passed

===== PR87097 final exact-head validation =====
VALIDATION_REF=9549c867a64fd87fdb9edba89142c9ce492dae9a
VALIDATION_COMMAND=pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/cli/local-agent-lifetime.ts src/cli/local-agent-lifetime.test.ts src/entry.ts src/index.ts
All matched files use the correct format.
VALIDATION_COMMAND=timeout 180s pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.cli.config.ts src/cli/local-agent-lifetime.test.ts --reporter=verbose
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  14 passed (14)
VALIDATION_COMMAND=pnpm build
VALIDATION_COMMAND=pnpm tsgo:core
PR87097_HEAD_VALIDATION=passed
PR87097_BEHAVIOR_PROOF=passed
run summary sync=0s command=12m41.728s total=12m41.811s sync_skipped=true exit=0
```

Observed result after fix: Current `main` completed the agent turn but did not exit before the outer timeout, so it returned `124` after 92s. The PR head completed the same agent turn, called the otel pre-exit hook, and exited `0` after 14s despite the deliberately ref'd background handle.

What was not tested: No changed-path behavior gap remains for this PR. This proof does not claim to fix every subsystem root handle; it proves the local-agent CLI lifetime workaround and the optional otel pre-exit hook under a real CLI invocation with a ref'd background handle.

target.title: fix(gateway): discover disk compaction checkpoints
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #68864.

- discover valid `{session}.checkpoint.<uuid>.jsonl` transcript files when `sessions.json` is missing `compactionCheckpoints`
- include disk-discovered checkpoints in `sessions.list` row count/latest preview and `sessions.compaction.list/get`
- allow branch/restore to operate on disk-discovered checkpoints, not just metadata-backed checkpoints

## Tests

- `PATH=/tmp/openclaw-corepack-shims:$PATH pnpm check:changed -- --base origin/main`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `node --import tsx -e '<inline script creating a real SessionManager transcript, checkpoint snapshot, then calling listSessionCompactionCheckpointsWithFilesAsync>'`
- attempted: `corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/server.sessions.compaction.test.ts -t "discovers checkpoint transcript files" --reporter=verbose` (stopped after the local Vitest/Rolldown build stayed in plugin timing output for over two minutes without reaching test assertions)
- attempted: `corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/gateway/session-compaction-checkpoints.test.ts -t "discovers valid checkpoint" --reporter=verbose` (same local Vitest/Rolldown build stall)

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Web UI/session APIs no longer report an empty checkpoint list when checkpoint transcript files exist on disk but `sessions.json` has no `compactionCheckpoints` metadata.

Real environment tested: Local Linux workspace on this branch with the OpenClaw monorepo at `origin/main` base `3a2220f0a5`.

Exact steps or command run after the patch:

```bash
node --import tsx -e 'created a real SessionManager session transcript, captured a .checkpoint.<uuid>.jsonl snapshot, omitted compactionCheckpoints metadata, called listSessionCompactionCheckpointsWithFilesAsync, and required one disk checkpoint with matching leaf/sessionFile'
```

Evidence after fix: Console output from the direct runtime command:

```text
{"discovered":1,"checkpointId":"0f65407f-a898-498e-bcb4-112096da3818","hasLeaf":true,"hasSessionFile":true}
```

Observed result after fix: The real transcript/checkpoint setup returned exactly one disk-discovered checkpoint and included both the pre-compaction leaf id and snapshot file path.

What was not tested: Local Vitest execution did not complete on this host because the test runner stayed in the Rolldown plugin build phase; this PR relies on GitHub CI for actual Vitest execution.

target.title: fix(plugin-state): evict current namespace on plugin row cap
target.body:
## Summary

- Make plugin-state shed old rows from the namespace that just grew before failing a plugin-wide row-limit write.
- Raise the plugin-state per-plugin row budget and Telegram persistent message-cache budget from 1000 to 3000 rows.
- Keep sibling namespaces protected; if the current namespace cannot free enough old rows, the write still fails atomically.
- Add regression coverage for the Telegram shape that failed in production, now sized against the 3000-row budget.

## Verification

All commands below were rerun on rebased head `feee577cb1` using the existing system Node install: `node v26.2.0` at `/usr/bin/node`.

- `node -v && which node` -> `v26.2.0`, `/usr/bin/node`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.sqlite.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.test.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.e2e.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.ts extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts`
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node_modules/.bin/vitest run src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.test.ts --reporter=dot --passWithNoTests=false` (`23 passed`)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.e2e.config.ts src/plugin-state/plugin-state-store.e2e.test.ts -t "enforces the per-plugin live-row cap" --reporter=dot` (`1 passed | 11 skipped`)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/telegram/src/message-cache.test.ts --reporter=dot --passWithNoTests=false` (`16 passed`)
- `pnpm run tsgo:core`
- `pnpm run tsgo:test:src`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Telegram plugin state reached the old 1000 live-row plugin budget (message-cache plus topic-name-cache), so a new topic/message cache write threw `PluginStateStoreError` and surfaced as a generic Telegram processing failure.

Budget after fix: plugin-state allows 3000 live rows per plugin, and Telegram persistent message-cache allows 3000 entries.

Real runtime probe: On rebased PR head `feee577cb1`, I ran a `tsx` probe with system Node `v26.2.0` at `/usr/bin/node` against a temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, using the actual `createPluginStateKeyedStore` runtime API and the real plugin-state SQLite store. The probe seeded 2989 `telegram.message-cache` rows plus 11 `telegram.topic-name-cache` rows, then registered one new message-cache row.

Runtime output:

```text
NODE_VERSION=v26.2.0
NODE_PATH=/usr/bin/node
PR_HEAD=feee577cb1
STATE_DIR=/tmp/<temp>
DB_PATH=$OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR/plugin-state/state.sqlite
PLUGIN_CAP=3000
SEEDED_BEFORE message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000
REGISTER_NEW result=success key=msg-new
AFTER message_cache=2989 topic_cache=11 total=3000
EVICTED_ORIGINAL_MESSAGE_KEYS=msg-0000
ASSERT new_message_present=true
ASSERT exactly_one_original_message_evicted=true
ASSERT topic_cache_preserved=true
ASSERT plugin_total_within_cap=true
DB_BYTES=585728
```

Evidence after fix: The real runtime probe successfully wrote the new Telegram message-cache row at the 3000-row plugin budget, evicted exactly one older message-cache row from the current namespace, preserved all 11 topic-name-cache rows, and kept the plugin total within cap.

Observed result after fix: Current-namespace cache growth no longer fails only because sibling plugin-state namespaces are also using rows, and the Telegram cache has a larger 3000-row runway.

What was not tested: A full live Telegram gateway rebuild on this PR head; production was manually unblocked by trimming old Telegram message-cache rows before this patch. The after-fix path was tested with the real plugin-state runtime API and SQLite store in an isolated runtime state directory.

target.title: feat(slack): render progress as native progress task cards
target.body:
## Summary
- Add Slack native progress task cards as an explicit opt-in feature for `channels.slack.streaming.mode="progress"` via `channels.slack.streaming.progress.nativeTaskCards=true`.
- Preserve existing progress-mode behavior by keeping the portable draft-preview path when the new opt-in is unset.
- Keep Slack native progress plan/task rendering and completion updates for opted-in accounts, including first running-tool visibility before final text through Slack `plan_update` / `task_update` stream chunks.
- Keep `nativeTaskCards` scoped to Slack config while preserving the public `SlackChannelStreamingConfig` type export and stable progress identity fields used by the Slack path.

## Maintainer review guide
- Default behavior should remain unchanged: Slack `mode="progress"` still uses the portable draft-preview path unless `channels.slack.streaming.progress.nativeTaskCards=true` is set.
- Config/API boundary: `nativeTaskCards` is Slack-only config (`src/config/types.slack.ts`, `src/config/zod-schema.providers-core.ts`) and is not part of the shared channel progress schema.
- Runtime boundary: `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts` gates the opted-in path, starts/appends/stops the Slack native stream, and keeps final replies in the same thread.
- Rendering boundary: `extensions/slack/src/progress-blocks.ts` converts progress lines into Slack `plan_update` / `task_update` chunks and keeps stable task ids when upstream events provide item/tool ids.
- Slack transport boundary: `extensions/slack/src/streaming.ts` extends the existing Slack stream helper to carry structured chunks alongside text.
- Best review path: check the opt-in gate, the chunk construction, the stream lifecycle/finalization path, then the focused dispatch/renderer tests listed below.

## Suggested follow-ups
- Stable identity for repeated same-name tools when upstream progress events do not provide item/tool ids. This PR preserves ids when present, but could be broadened in a separate follow-up if maintainers want stricter repeated-tool identity.
- More precise native task finalization for failed commands followed by a normal assistant reply. This PR finalizes visible in-progress native tasks for the opt-in path, but deeper command-outcome propagation could be handled in a separate follow-up if maintainers want that behavior tightened.

## Verification
- `git diff --check HEAD~2..HEAD`
- `node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --check`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-dispatch-streaming-$$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.streaming.test.ts` (26 passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-dispatch-preview-rerun-$$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts` (44 passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-config-schema-$$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/schema.test.ts` (39 passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-slack-progress-blocks-$$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/progress-blocks.test.ts` (19 passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-slack-streaming-$$ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/streaming.test.ts` (19 passed)
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-pr82258-rebased node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts extensions/slack/src/progress-blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts` (211 passed)
- After commit-history cleanup: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-pr82258-clean-history node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts extensions/slack/src/progress-blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts` (211 passed); cleaned head `1725061aba3b0a7c1f0d2b93cd0d1db5ed5ea040` had the same tree `96359c125f93975d3e9de2c3d35d73a03359e81a` as the live-proof head.
- Patch identity rank-up at head `d2bec4654955d8012aa2ffffb80389dc999a19e8`: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-pr82258-patch-rankup node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/progress-blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts` (107 passed).
- Rebase/CI-fix head `8f81b320823dd7b1818bd688d71b29a6e46820cb`: `node --import tsx scripts/generate-bundled-channel-config-metadata.ts --check` passed; `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-pr82258-ci-fix node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/plugin-sdk/channel-streaming.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts extensions/slack/src/progress-blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.streaming.test.ts` passed; `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=0 pnpm check:test-types` passed; `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK=0 pnpm tsgo:prod` passed; `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` passed.
- Codex review fix at head `851fa6afc72afebd731fc2aaa972e8a4416b90f4`: `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-pr82258-codex-review-red node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts -t "does not start a text stream for native progress mode when no progress card exists"` failed before the fix because `startSlackStream` was called with final text, then passed after the fix; `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-pr82258-codex-review-fix node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/progress-blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/streaming.test.ts` passed (4 files, 111 tests); `git diff --check` passed.
- Scope cleanup at current head `275035024c814cb6b70c00f99eade86ac7b49f52`: rebased onto `origin/main` at `a13468320c`; removed the unrelated OpenRouter lint cleanup from the PR diff; `git diff origin/main...HEAD -- extensions/openrouter/provider-routing.ts src/commands/status.summary.redaction.test.ts` is empty; `OPENCLAW_VITEST_FS_MODULE_CACHE_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-vitest-pr82258-rebased-scope-clean node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.preview-fallback.test.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.streaming.test.ts extensions/slack/src/progress-blocks.test.ts extensions/slack/src/streaming.test.ts` passed (4 files, 111 tests); `git diff --check` passed. Current-head GitHub checks had no failures at the latest snapshot, with main CI and CodeQL still queued/pending.

## Visual proof
Video: 
[Screencast From 2026-05-20 11-38-07.webm](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/da5eccc9-3061-4bec-8069-e2de24f698a0)

Shows the new opt-in Slack native progress experience:
  - A native Slack plan/task card appears while OpenClaw is still working.
  - The card updates as the tool-backed trip comparison progresses.
  - The task card completes before the final answer is posted.
  - The final answer lands in the same Slack thread after the native progress card.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Slack native progress task cards are opt-in through Slack-only `channels.slack.streaming.progress.nativeTaskCards=true`; existing Slack progress mode remains on the portable draft-preview path unless that flag is set. The opted-in native path sends Slack stream chunks (`plan_update` / `task_update`) instead of replacing full Block Kit messages.
Real environment tested: local source-run OpenClaw gateway from this PR branch at proof head `f8d834382cc7f429f73d03cf1095ea46831d216b`; cleaned head `1725061aba3b0a7c1f0d2b93cd0d1db5ed5ea040` had the same tree `96359c125f93975d3e9de2c3d35d73a03359e81a`. Current head `275035024c814cb6b70c00f99eade86ac7b49f52` includes the patch-progress identity rank-up, the post-rebase Slack CI/type fixes, the Codex review fix that keeps final-only native-progress turns on normal final delivery instead of Slack text streaming, and a scope cleanup that removes the unrelated OpenRouter lint change from the PR diff after rebasing onto latest `origin/main`. Slack workspace `4ok`, channel `C0ASNARACMT`, test sender message mentioning OpenClaw test app using OpenClaw test app credentials. For this proof, the source-dev Slack config was in `streaming.mode="progress"` with `streaming.progress.nativeTaskCards=true`.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: started the local source-run gateway with OpenClaw test app credentials and a redacted HTTP-level Slack stream recorder, verified `/healthz` returned `{"ok":true,"status":"live"}` and logs showed `slack socket mode connected`; ran ingestion smoke marker `SMOKE-HEAD3-20260520T090802Z`; then ran `MARKER=SEQ38-HEAD-20260520T090828Z STREAM_ARTIFACT=/tmp/openclaw-SEQ38-HEAD-20260520T090741Z-stream-calls.jsonl ARTIFACT=/tmp/openclaw-SEQ38-HEAD-20260520T090828Z-slack-proof.json /tmp/openclaw-slack-proof.py`.
Evidence after fix: Slack thread `https://4okworkspace.slack.com/archives/C0ASNARACMT/p1779268109549229`; proof artifact `/tmp/openclaw-SEQ38-HEAD-20260520T090828Z-slack-proof.json`; redacted stream-call artifact `/tmp/openclaw-SEQ38-HEAD-20260520T090741Z-stream-calls.jsonl`; pass flags were `final_text_seen=true` and `stream_chunk_proof=true`. The stream artifact shows `chat.startStream` at `2026-05-20T09:08:43.966Z` with `task_display_mode=plan` and chunks `plan_update` + `task_update`, where the task status was `in_progress`, followed by multiple `chat.appendStream` requests carrying `plan_update` + `task_update` chunks before `chat.stopStream` at `2026-05-20T09:09:10.794Z`.
Observed result after fix: OpenClaw test app consumed the test sender prompt, Slack native progress was delivered through stream chunks while the command was still running, the native tasks later updated to `complete`, and the Slack thread received the exact final marker reply `FINAL-SEQ38-HEAD-20260520T090828Z`.
What was not tested: screenshot/video capture of the transient Slack UI; `conversations.replies` does not expose pre-stop stream chunk contents reliably, so proof uses redacted Slack stream request evidence plus final thread readback. Repeated same-name tool calls without upstream stable IDs may still collapse, and a failed command followed by a normal final assistant reply may finalize the native task as complete rather than error; both are intentionally left out of scope to keep this PR limited to the opt-in Slack-native progress feature.

## Summary

ClawSweeper review identified that the original fix only included `mediaPath`/`mediaType`/`mediaPaths`/`mediaTypes` but omitted `mediaUrl` and `mediaUrls`, which are available in `FinalizedMsgContext` and needed by plugins handling URL-based media references.

**Key change**: Added `mediaUrl` and `mediaUrls` fields to `CanonicalInboundMessageHookContext`, `deriveInboundMessageHookContext`, `toPluginInboundClaimEvent`, and `toPluginMessageReceivedEvent`.

## Changes
- `src/hooks/message-hook-mappers.ts`: Add mediaUrl/mediaUrls to type, derive function, and both event mappers
- `src/hooks/message-hook-mappers.test.ts`: Add test coverage for all six media metadata fields

target.title: fix(feishu): fall back to post mode when markdown tables exceed card limit
target.body:
## Problem

When an agent's reply contains more than 5 Markdown tables, the Feishu plugin detects tables and automatically sends the reply as an interactive card. However, the Feishu card API limits each card to at most 5 table components (`ErrCode: 11310; card table number over limit`). Messages exceeding this limit enter the delivery queue and cannot be successfully delivered.

This affects both card-mode decision points:
- Auto-reply (`reply-dispatcher.ts`) — card message dispatch
- Outbound (`outbound.ts`) — `sendOutboundText` / `sendText` card decision

The issue also occurs when `renderMode` is explicitly set to `"card"` (not just `"auto"`).

## Solution

Added `withinCardTableLimit()` helper that counts markdown tables. This check is applied at every card-mode decision point in the modified code paths, including `renderMode: "card"`, since the Feishu limit cannot be overridden by configuration.

The existing `shouldUseCard()` is kept as-is for detecting markdown features (code blocks, tables), while `withinCardTableLimit()` provides a separate hard constraint (≤5 tables).

When card mode is rejected, the message falls back to post mode, which uses `convertMarkdownTables(text, "code")` — a reliable, already existing code path.

## Changes

- `extensions/feishu/src/reply-dispatcher.ts`: Added `withinCardTableLimit()` helper. Modified `useCard` decision to check table count before card mode. Applied to both `"card"` and `"auto"` render modes.
- `extensions/feishu/src/outbound.ts`: Same change, applied at both card-decision points (`sendOutboundText` and `sendText`).

## Testing

- Verified that 5 tables → card mode (works)
- Verified that 6 tables → API returns `ErrCode: 11310` (confirmed hard limit is exactly 5)
- Verified that with the fix, 6 tables → falls back to post mode, message delivered successfully
- Verified the fix works even with `renderMode: "card"` explicitly configured

## Backward Compatibility

- No change for messages with ≤5 tables
- No change for messages without tables
- No change for `renderMode: "auto"` behavior with code blocks (still uses card mode)


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Feishu card API limits each card to at most 5 table components (ErrCode 11310). Messages with 6+ markdown tables fail and enter the delivery queue.
- **Real environment tested**: Production OpenClaw instance running feishu extension. Same setup that hit the original 18-table delivery-queue failure.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: Built patched feishu extension, restarted gateway. Sent a test message with 7 markdown tables via feishu DM. Also verified 5 tables still uses card mode.
- **Evidence after fix**:
  1. Real-world failure from delivery queue (18 tables):
  ```
  2026-05-26T20:02:29 [delivery-recovery] Retry failed:
  Feishu card send failed: {
    "http_status": 400,
    "feishu_code": 230099,
    "feishu_msg": "Failed to create card content,
      ext=ErrCode: 11310;
      ErrMsg: card table number over limit;
      ErrorValue: table;"
  }
  ```
  2. Verification 6 tables also fails (before fix):
  ```
  2026-05-26T20:50:50 [feishu] feishu final reply failed:
  Error: Feishu card send failed: {
    "http_status": 400,
    "feishu_code": 230099,
    "feishu_msg": "Failed to create card content,
      ext=ErrCode: 11310;
      ErrMsg: card table number over limit;
      ErrorValue: table;"
  }
  ```
  3. Feishu official docs confirm the limit: https://open.feishu.cn/document/feishu-cards/card-json-v2-components/content-components/table?lang=zh-CN — "单张卡片最多支持放置五个表格组件"
- **Observed result after fix**: With the fix applied, messages with 7 tables are successfully delivered via post mode (tables converted to code blocks). No delivery queue entries. Messages with ≤5 tables continue to use card mode normally.
- **What was not tested**: Streaming card path (Card Kit API `cardkit/v1/cards`). This PR only affects static interactive cards (`msg_type: "interactive"`). Streaming cards use a separate code path and are unrelated to this fix.

target.title: feat: add scoped mention pattern policy
target.body:
## Summary
- add a shared scoped mention-pattern policy for global, agent, and provider/channel overrides
- apply configured mention-pattern gating across channel runtimes while preserving native mentions
- document raw regex-body syntax and regenerate config/help metadata

## Notes
- Recreated from a clean `origin/main` branch after #70846 was closed as dirty; this branch has been rebased on current `origin/main` and generated baselines refreshed after upstream movement.

## Validation
- `pnpm --config.minimum-release-age=0 install --lockfile-only --ignore-scripts=false --config.engine-strict=false --config.enable-pre-post-scripts=true --config.side-effects-cache=true`
- `pnpm plugin-sdk:api:check`
- `pnpm config:channels:check`
- `pnpm config:docs:check`
- `pnpm test extensions/qa-channel/src/inbound.test.ts src/channels/mention-pattern-policy.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-context.body.test.ts`
- `pnpm check:changed`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Scoped mention-pattern policy now resolves the effective provider/agent/conversation policy so configured regex mentions are enabled only for explicitly allowed conversations in deny mode and remain disabled for denied or unlisted conversations. Latest follow-up rebased onto current `origin/main`, resolved refreshed Plugin SDK baseline conflicts, and reran the exact-head behavior proof.
- **Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw PR checkout on host `patrick`, branch `feature/mention-pattern-policy-clean`, head `f435da82e7d1c0824d3474c5c5821378f7762281`, running the real source resolver, Telegram group peer id helper, mention matcher, and inbound mention decision code through Node/tsx against redacted OpenClaw-style config/message facts. This does not exercise live external delivery.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:** `cd /home/patrick/projects/openclaw-mention-pattern-policy && HEAD_SHA=f435da82e7d1c0824d3474c5c5821378f7762281 HEAD_SHORT=f435da82e7 node --import tsx /tmp/pr70864-proof-c770334.mjs`
- **Evidence after fix:** Terminal output from the real source smoke:

```text
OpenClaw scoped mention policy Telegram media pre-download proof (f435da82e7, f435da82e7d1c0824d3474c5c5821378f7762281)
{"topic":7,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:7","mentionPatternsEnabled":true,"wasMentioned":true,"shouldSkip":false,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":9,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:9","mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
{"topic":11,"conversationId":"-1001234567893:topic:11","mentionPatternsEnabled":false,"wasMentioned":false,"shouldSkip":true,"skipReason":null}
```

- **Observed result after fix:** In inherited `deny` mode, the media pre-download path honors the Telegram account policy rather than the global provider fallback: account-allowed topic `-1001234567893:topic:7` enables configured regex mention `bot` and media pre-download is not skipped, while account-denied topic `:topic:9` and unlisted topic `:topic:11` disable configured regex mentions and skip unaddressed group media before download.
- **What was not tested:** Full live external Telegram delivery was not exercised in this proof; CI and targeted channel tests cover the integration paths.

target.title: fix(hooks): include media metadata in toPluginMessageReceivedEvent
target.body:
## Summary

ClawSweeper review identified that the original fix only included `mediaPath`/`mediaType`/`mediaPaths`/`mediaTypes` but omitted `mediaUrl` and `mediaUrls`, which are available in `FinalizedMsgContext` and needed by plugins handling URL-based media references.

**Key change**: Added `mediaUrl` and `mediaUrls` fields to `CanonicalInboundMessageHookContext`, `deriveInboundMessageHookContext`, `toPluginInboundClaimEvent`, and `toPluginMessageReceivedEvent`.

## Changes
- `src/hooks/message-hook-mappers.ts`: Add mediaUrl/mediaUrls to type, derive function, and both event mappers
- `src/hooks/message-hook-mappers.test.ts`: Add test coverage for all six media metadata fields

target.title: fix(config): skip .openclaw append when OPENCLAW_HOME already names a state dir (#45765)
target.body:
## Summary

Setting `OPENCLAW_HOME=~/.openclaw` produces the nested directory `~/.openclaw/.openclaw/` and onboarding writes the config file to the wrong place. Reporter on Linux Kylin V10 / aarch64 (and macOS 15.4) documented a 100% reproduction rate on OpenClaw 2026.3.12 (#45765). This change adds a closed-set nesting guard so that when `OPENCLAW_HOME` already names a known state directory (`.openclaw` or the legacy `.clawdbot`), the explicit home is used as the state/config directory directly instead of being treated as a parent that needs a `.openclaw` subdirectory.

## Root Cause

- `src/config/paths.ts:60-89` — `resolveStateDir()` calls `newStateDir(effectiveHomedir)` which unconditionally appends `NEW_STATE_DIRNAME = ".openclaw"` to whatever home dir comes back from `resolveRequiredHomeDir()`. When the operator explicitly set `OPENCLAW_HOME=~/.openclaw`, the resolved home is already `~/.openclaw`, so the helper produces `~/.openclaw/.openclaw`.
- `src/utils.ts:119-141` — `resolveConfigDir()` does the same: `path.join(resolveRequiredHomeDir(env, homedir), ".openclaw")`.
- `src/config/paths.ts:272-296` — `resolveDefaultConfigCandidates()` builds candidate config paths through the same `newStateDir(effectiveHomedir)` / `legacyStateDirs(effectiveHomedir)` helpers and emits the nested `~/.openclaw/.openclaw/openclaw.json` path as the canonical first candidate. Because `resolveConfigPathCandidate()` returns the first existing path from that list, onboarding writes the config to the shadow nested directory and every subsequent read picks it up too.
- Execution path: `openclaw onboard` → `resolveConfigPathCandidate` → `resolveDefaultConfigCandidates` → `newStateDir(effectiveHomedir)` → `path.join(~/.openclaw, ".openclaw")` → writes config to `~/.openclaw/.openclaw/openclaw.json`.

This follows clawsweeper's direction on #45765:
*"Add a narrow known-state-directory basename guard for explicit `OPENCLAW_HOME` in state and config resolution, preserve documented parent-home behavior, and cover `.openclaw` plus supported legacy state directory names with regression tests."*

## Changes

- `src/config/paths.ts`:
  - Export `ALL_STATE_DIRNAMES` (`ReadonlySet<string>`) combining `NEW_STATE_DIRNAME` (`.openclaw`) and `LEGACY_STATE_DIRNAMES` (`.clawdbot`). Any new legacy state-dir name added to `LEGACY_STATE_DIRNAMES` joins the guard automatically.
  - Export `isExplicitOpenClawHomeStateDir(env, resolvedHome)` — returns `true` only when `env.OPENCLAW_HOME?.trim()` is non-empty AND `path.basename(resolvedHome)` is in `ALL_STATE_DIRNAMES`. The implicit `$HOME` / `$USERPROFILE` path keeps the documented parent-home convention even when the OS home happens to be named `.openclaw`; we opt in only when the operator was explicit.
  - `resolveStateDir()`: if the guard matches, return the effective home directly (skip `newStateDir(effectiveHomedir)`).
  - `resolveDefaultConfigCandidates()`: if the guard matches, use `[home]` as the single default directory list instead of `[newStateDir, ...legacyStateDirs]`, so candidate paths become `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` (and the legacy filename variants under the same dir) instead of `~/.openclaw/.openclaw/openclaw.json`.
- `src/utils.ts`:
  - Import `isExplicitOpenClawHomeStateDir` from `./config/paths.js` (no circular dependency — `paths.ts` does not import from `utils.ts`).
  - `resolveConfigDir()`: if the guard matches, return the explicit home directly instead of `path.join(home, ".openclaw")`.
- `src/config/paths.test.ts`: add 6 new regression cases under a new `OPENCLAW_HOME nesting guard (#45765)` describe block covering: `ALL_STATE_DIRNAMES` membership; `isExplicitOpenClawHomeStateDir` true/false matrix; `resolveStateDir` + `resolveDefaultConfigCandidates` for `.openclaw` and `.clawdbot`; non-state-dir `OPENCLAW_HOME` preserves existing append behavior; implicit `$HOME=~/.openclaw` (no `OPENCLAW_HOME`) keeps the documented parent-home convention.
- `src/utils.test.ts`: add 3 new regression cases covering `resolveConfigDir` for `.openclaw`, `.clawdbot`, and the non-state-dir `OPENCLAW_HOME` regression check.

## Reproduction (before fix)

Source-reproducible against current `upstream/main` (`12e5876903`):

~~~text
$ node --import tsx ./smoke-before-fix.mjs
OPENCLAW_HOME=/home/user/.openclaw
  resolveStateDir   -> /home/user/.openclaw/.openclaw   (BUG: nested)
  resolveConfigDir  -> /home/user/.openclaw/.openclaw   (BUG: nested)
  candidates[0]     -> /home/user/.openclaw/.openclaw/openclaw.json   (BUG: writes shadow file)
~~~

## Verification (after fix)

~~~text
$ node --import tsx ./smoke-45765.mjs
PASS: OPENCLAW_HOME=/home/user/.openclaw (#45765 case)
  resolveStateDir       -> /home/user/.openclaw           (fixed; no nesting)
  resolveConfigDir      -> /home/user/.openclaw           (fixed; no nesting)
  candidates[0]         -> /home/user/.openclaw/openclaw.json   (writes the canonical path)
  no nested .openclaw   OK
PASS: OPENCLAW_HOME=/home/user/.clawdbot (legacy)
  resolveStateDir       -> /home/user/.clawdbot
  resolveConfigDir      -> /home/user/.clawdbot
  candidates[0]         -> /home/user/.clawdbot/openclaw.json
  no nested .openclaw   OK
PASS: OPENCLAW_HOME=/srv/openclaw-home (non-state-dir; existing behavior must remain)
  resolveStateDir       -> /srv/openclaw-home/.openclaw   (unchanged; parent-home convention preserved)
  resolveConfigDir      -> /srv/openclaw-home/.openclaw
  candidates[0]         -> /srv/openclaw-home/.openclaw/openclaw.json
  no nested .openclaw   OK

RESULTS: 3 pass / 0 fail / 3 total
~~~

## Test

- `pnpm test src/config/paths.test.ts` — 29 passed (incl. 6 new `OPENCLAW_HOME nesting guard` regression cases)
- `pnpm test src/utils.test.ts` — 18 passed (incl. 3 new `OPENCLAW_HOME nesting guard` regression cases)
- `pnpm test src/infra/home-dir.test.ts` — 27 passed (no regressions in the underlying home-dir resolver)
- `pnpm tsgo:core` — passed (production typecheck clean)

## Notes

- Scope: 4 files, +140/-2 LOC. Production surface is the 2 resolvers plus the new exported helper and constant in `src/config/paths.ts`; the rest is the expanded regression test coverage.
- Compatibility: preserved across all existing entry points.
  - `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR` / `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH` explicit overrides take priority before the guard (existing `resolveStateDir` / `resolveConfigDir` early returns are unchanged).
  - `OPENCLAW_HOME=/srv/openclaw-home` and any other non-state-dir basename still resolves to `/srv/openclaw-home/.openclaw` — the existing `"uses OPENCLAW_HOME for default state/config locations"` test (`paths.test.ts:151`) and `"prefers OPENCLAW_HOME over HOME for default state/config locations"` test (`paths.test.ts:158`) continue to pass without modification.
  - Implicit `$HOME=~/.openclaw` (no `OPENCLAW_HOME` set) keeps the documented `$HOME/.openclaw` convention — the guard short-circuits only when `env.OPENCLAW_HOME?.trim()` is non-empty.
  - Tilde-expanded explicit homes (`OPENCLAW_HOME=~/.openclaw`) work because `resolveEffectiveHomeDir` already runs through `expandHomePrefix` + `path.resolve` before the guard sees the resolved absolute path.
  - Symlinks: `path.basename(resolvedHome)` operates on the symlink name, not its target, so the guard matches the operator's chosen path name. This is the right semantic ("what did the user ask for?") and matches the analysis already in the prior closed attempt #45793.
- This is a narrower replacement for the closed-stale #45793 (also from a separate contributor, technically correct, lost to no maintainer engagement). The shape is the same; the new test coverage explicitly pins the implicit-`HOME` case so the guard cannot accidentally widen.
- Out-of-scope: `OPENCLAW_HOME` poisoning (already handled by `normalize()` in `src/infra/home-dir.ts:4`) and `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/path/.openclaw` style explicit-state-dir overrides (those already short-circuit before the guard at line 65).

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: Onboarding with `OPENCLAW_HOME=~/.openclaw` writes the config file to the wrong place (`~/.openclaw/.openclaw/openclaw.json`) and produces a nested directory. After the fix, the config is written to the canonical `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` location.
- Real environment tested: Local checkout at `../openclaw-45765` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14 with the patched `src/config/paths.ts` and `src/utils.ts`. Smoke run exercises the actual production `resolveStateDir`, `resolveConfigDir`, and `resolveDefaultConfigCandidates` exports through `node --import tsx`, no test runner, no mocks. PR head: `44b9b82478`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `git checkout fix/openclaw-home-state-dir-nesting && pnpm install`
  - `pnpm test src/config/paths.test.ts` (29 passed)
  - `pnpm test src/utils.test.ts` (18 passed)
  - `pnpm test src/infra/home-dir.test.ts` (27 passed)
  - `pnpm tsgo:core` (EXIT=0)
  - `node --import tsx ./smoke-45765.mjs` against the three observed shapes
- Evidence after fix:
~~~text
PR head 44b9b82478
$ pnpm test src/config/paths.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  29 passed (29)
   Duration  1.96s

$ pnpm test src/utils.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  18 passed (18)
   Duration  1.97s

$ pnpm test src/infra/home-dir.test.ts
 Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests  27 passed (27)
   Duration  331ms

$ pnpm tsgo:core
EXIT=0
~~~
- Observed result after fix: with `OPENCLAW_HOME=~/.openclaw` set, `resolveStateDir` / `resolveConfigDir` both return the explicit home directly and `resolveDefaultConfigCandidates` lists `~/.openclaw/openclaw.json` as the canonical first candidate — no `~/.openclaw/.openclaw/...` candidate is produced anywhere in the list. With `OPENCLAW_HOME=/srv/openclaw-home` (non-state-dir basename), behavior is unchanged: `~/.openclaw` is still appended.
- What was not tested: a live `openclaw onboard` wizard run on Linux Kylin V10 / aarch64 (the reporter's environment). The behavior is source-reproducible from the patched production code through `node --import tsx` and is unit-covered by the new regression tests; a live onboarding run would only exercise the same call chain.

Closes #45765

target.title: fix: refuse local tsgo under host pressure
target.body:
## Summary
- refuse local native type-aware checks by default on developer/agent hosts
- cover `tsgo`, type-aware `oxlint`, and sharded type-aware `oxlint`
- cover direct raw `tsgo` helper scripts: extension package boundary, core boundary, and tsgo profiling
- keep metadata commands cheap and unblocked (`--help`, `--version`, etc.)
- keep CI/GitHub Actions unblocked so broad native checks run online instead of on constrained hosts
- preserve explicit break-glass overrides for local runs
- keep pressure refusal and effective heavy-check temp-dir checks from the earlier patch
- rebase onto current `main` and preserve upstream serial shard support

## Why
A constrained Raspberry Pi/agent host repeatedly became unstable when native type-aware checks (`tsgo`/`tsgolint` via type-aware oxlint) ran locally. Serialization and throttling reduce concurrency, but they still let the same memory-hungry native check run on the same fragile host. That is the wrong failure mode.

This PR now makes the safer default explicit: local wrappers and repo helper scripts refuse native type-aware checks unless the operator opts in. CI, Blacksmith/Testbox, or another remote worker should own those broad checks.

## Behavior
Local native type-aware checks refuse by default when local heavy-check policy is active and mode is not `full`:

- `scripts/run-tsgo.mjs`
- `scripts/run-oxlint.mjs` when it runs type-aware oxlint, including explicit file-target runs
- `scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs` before artifact prep or child shard spawning
- `scripts/check-extension-package-tsc-boundary.mjs`
- `scripts/check-tsgo-core-boundary.mjs`
- `scripts/profile-tsgo.mjs`

Bypasses / allowed cases:

- GitHub Actions / CI are not blocked
- metadata-only commands are not blocked
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=full` allows the local run
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_FORCE=1` allows the local run
- `OPENCLAW_ALLOW_LOCAL_NATIVE_TYPECHECK=1` allows the local run
- child processes already covered by a parent heavy-check lock can continue without self-deadlock

Existing pressure checks still refuse local heavy checks when headroom is too low:

- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_MIN_MEM_AVAILABLE_BYTES` defaults to `1.5 GiB`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_MIN_TMP_AVAILABLE_BYTES` defaults to `1 GiB`
- `OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_MAX_TMP_USED_PERCENT` defaults to `80`

## GitHub CI note
GitHub CI means these checks run online on GitHub/runner machines after pushing the branch, not on the local Raspberry Pi. The PR gets pass/fail status from those remote runners.

## Test Plan
Final head: `ce15395324be19fd9a07db68a8e4c7c4fe4c35e2`

Local checks intentionally stayed narrow and refusal-only. Broad lint/test/type-aware gates are left to GitHub CI.

Narrow local syntax/refusal probes run after the latest patch:

- `node --check scripts/check-extension-package-tsc-boundary.mjs`
- `node --check scripts/profile-tsgo.mjs`
- `node --check scripts/check-tsgo-core-boundary.mjs`
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled node scripts/check-extension-package-tsc-boundary.mjs --mode=compile`
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled node scripts/profile-tsgo.mjs core --json`
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled node scripts/check-tsgo-core-boundary.mjs`

Previously passed locally before this direct-script follow-up:

- syntax checks for `run-tsgo`, `run-oxlint`, `run-oxlint-shards`, and local heavy-check runtime
- scoped formatter/lint checks on touched wrapper files
- `OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=throttled pnpm test test/scripts/local-heavy-check-runtime.test.ts test/scripts/run-oxlint.test.ts --run`
- result: `Test Files 2 passed`, `Tests 34 passed`

## Real behavior proof
- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Local OpenClaw script entry points that can start native `tsgo`/`tsgolint` now refuse on developer/agent hosts by default, while sharded oxlint metadata commands (`--help`, `--version`, etc.) delegate directly without parent artifact preparation, lock acquisition, shard spawning, or heavy temp-dir creation.
- **Real environment tested:** Raspberry Pi OpenClaw checkout on branch `fix/local-tsgo-pressure-guard`, head `00540345e8e1`, rebased onto `origin/main` `c4b9f54b4628`; local env explicitly removed `CI`/`GITHUB_ACTIONS` for refusal probes.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
git diff --check

env -u CI -u GITHUB_ACTIONS OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE= OPENCLAW_LOCAL_HEAVY_CHECK_TMPDIR=.artifacts/proof-heavy-tmp node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs

env -u CI -u GITHUB_ACTIONS OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE= OPENCLAW_LOCAL_HEAVY_CHECK_TMPDIR=.artifacts/proof-heavy-tmp node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs --help

test ! -e .artifacts/proof-heavy-tmp

env -u CI -u GITHUB_ACTIONS OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE= OPENCLAW_LOCAL_HEAVY_CHECK_TMPDIR=.artifacts/proof-heavy-tmp node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs scripts/run-tsgo.mjs

env -u CI -u GITHUB_ACTIONS OPENCLAW_LOCAL_CHECK_MODE=full OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_MIN_MEM_AVAILABLE_BYTES=999999999999999 OPENCLAW_LOCAL_HEAVY_CHECK_TMPDIR=.artifacts/proof-heavy-tmp node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs --project config/tsconfig/root.json
```

- **Evidence after fix:**

```text
HEAD=00540345e8e1
git diff --check: exit=0

[default sharded refusal]
exit=1
Refusing to start sharded type-aware oxlint on this local host.
Native type-aware checks can exhaust constrained developer/agent machines; run them in GitHub CI, Blacksmith/Testbox, or another remote worker instead.
Arguments: <default project run>
If you really mean to run this locally, rerun with OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_FORCE=1 or OPENCLAW_ALLOW_LOCAL_NATIVE_TYPECHECK=1.

[metadata delegation / no parent temp dir]
exit=0
Usage: [-c=<./.oxlintrc.json>] [PATH]...
Basic Configuration
    -c, --config=<./.oxlintrc.json>  Oxlint configuration file

test ! -e .artifacts/proof-heavy-tmp: exit=0

[explicit file-target delegation]
exit=1
Refusing to start type-aware oxlint on this local host.
Native type-aware checks can exhaust constrained developer/agent machines; run them in GitHub CI, Blacksmith/Testbox, or another remote worker instead.
Arguments: scripts/run-tsgo.mjs --type-aware --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.json --report-unused-disable-directives-severity error --threads=1
If you really mean to run this locally, rerun with OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_FORCE=1 or OPENCLAW_ALLOW_LOCAL_NATIVE_TYPECHECK=1.

[tsgo pressure refusal]
exit=1
Refusing to start a local heavy check because this host is already under pressure:
- MemAvailable 5.4 GiB is below 909 TiB
Wait for pressure to clear, free temp space, or rerun with OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_FORCE=1 if you really mean it.
```

- **Observed result after fix:** The current head refuses the default sharded type-aware oxlint run locally before any shard work; metadata-only `--help` delegates directly and leaves `.artifacts/proof-heavy-tmp` absent; an explicit file target delegates to the narrow oxlint wrapper and refuses there instead of spawning the sharded parent path; `run-tsgo` refuses under simulated host pressure before native type-aware work starts.
- **What was not tested:** Broad local CI was intentionally not run on the Raspberry Pi. Full lint/test/type-aware validation is delegated to GitHub CI/remote runners.
- **What changed because of the evidence:** The proof now covers current head `00540345e8e1` and the latest sharded oxlint refusal/setup behavior that ClawSweeper flagged as stale.
